When I reported back for my second semester, I didn’t imagine how things would unfold. I simply came back ready to study, serve, and grow. Yet step by step, God aligned my path in ways I could never have planned.

I began by helping in the Christian Union hospitality ministry—serving quietly, learning humility, and enjoying fellowship. Then came the Peer Counsellors Club elections. It was a close contest, and I won by a slight margin against my rival. That victory wasn’t just about numbers; it was a reminder that God’s favor can tip the scales even when the odds feel tight. Being entrusted with leadership as chairperson taught me that service often opens doors to responsibility, and God elevates in His timing.

But the alignment didn’t stop there. Through work study opportunities across both semesters, I was able to save and purchase a powerful handset with pods—tools that now keep me productive and inspired on campus. The PM Club nominated me as their treasurer, a “double portion” blessing reminiscent of Elijah’s story. And in the midst of it all, a sponsor spoiled us with a chicken meal one Saturday afternoon—such enjoyment for comrades used to simple chapo beans. These small yet meaningful provisions reminded me that God sprinkles joy along the journey.

Even in my professional path, He aligned things perfectly. I secured an attachment close to home, with a stipend included—something I never imagined would work out so well. Truly, what a wonderful God we serve. He not only opens doors of leadership and provision, but also ensures that every detail falls into place at the right time.

Looking back, I see a clear thread: service, leadership, provision, enjoyment, and career—all aligned by God’s hand.

And so I close with this encouragement: To anyone out there who may be doubting, I urge you to keep trusting in Him. At the right time, He will make it happen. You may not see how the pieces will align, but God’s timing is always perfect.
